intro:

However, I can give you information about a different plant called Euodia, also known as Euodia subgibbosa or Euporteria subgibbosa is sometimes confused with this one, but Euodia is actually Euodia subgibbosa. 

Euodia subgibbosa is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to China and other parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects.

The medicinal properties of Euodia subgibbosa have been attributed to its ability to relieve fever, reduce inflammation, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and cough. Its health benefits also include potential anti-cancer properties and the ability to protect against cardiovascular disease. 

In traditional Chinese medicine, Euodia subgibbosa is prepared as a decoction, where the plant material is boiled in water to release its active compounds. It can also be prepared as a tincture, where the plant material is extracted in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
